steps.title: Identify the subject-verb boundary
steps.content: The subject is “the Huntington Library in California” (with “in California” modifying it), and the main verb is “received.” The blank occurs directly between them.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
No punctuation should separate a subject from its verb. The result is correct because it places no punctuation after “California,” allowing the subject “the Huntington Library in California” to flow directly into “received.” The other choices all insert punctuation that breaks this rule.

That is the correct answer.
